data.description:
## Why
Every gate row in epic pastoral-aquatic-viper adjudicates against ground truth that does not yet exist in frozen form. The report's §5 claims (16/505 post-claim edits, 3/8 review misses in one defect class) must be re-verified, not trusted, and the finding-level TP/FP labels needed by E2/E3/E6 and R1/R7 need a committed, versioned corpus.

## What
Freeze two ground-truth corpora under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ with a versioned README: (a) OUTCOME corpus - all ~527 reviewed tickets labeled with post-claim-edit class, reopens, force-closes, completion-verifier FAILs, and review round counts; (b) FINDING-ADJUDICATION corpus - stratified ~300 surfaced + ~100 dropped plan findings, panel-adjudicated TP/FP, extending the code_review_adjudication.jsonl pattern. Re-derive the §5 event history from git objects across all refs of the tickets branch (git rev-list --objects --all + cat-file; the on-disk .tickets-tracker/ is compacted) - method in report §5; ephemeral prior artifacts may exist at /Users/joeoakhart/.claude/jobs/e5b41784/tmp/ but the corpus must be reproducible without them.

## Scope
- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ (new: outcome corpus, adjudication corpus, README with schema + provenance + regeneration commands).
- A mining script committed alongside (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/), reusing corpus_sample.py conventions.
- NO gate/source changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The outcome corpus is committed with one row per reviewed ticket carrying: post-claim-edit class, reopen count, force-close flag, completion-verifier FAIL count, review round count; the README documents schema and the exact regeneration commands.
- [ ] The §5 headline claims are re-verified against the frozen corpus and the ticket comment records agreement or corrected numbers (16/505 edits; the 3-miss defect class on dc58/db7b/5886).
- [ ] The finding-adjudication corpus is committed with ~300 surfaced + ~100 dropped findings (stratified by criterion and decision), each with a TP/FP label and rationale, following the code_review_adjudication.jsonl pattern.
- [ ] Inter-rater reliability on a >=50-finding double-labeled subset reaches kappa >= 0.7, recorded in the README; below 0.7 the labels are declared unusable and the ticket documents the failed validity gate instead of shipping labels.
data.title: E1: freeze plan-review ground-truth corpora (outcome + finding adjudication, kappa gate)
